title: fix: repair WAV header size fields in chat completions audio path
author: YoannDev90
pr_body_excerpt:
The /v1/chat/completions endpoint with audio.format: "wav" returns base64-encoded WAV where both the RIFF file size and data chunk size are set to 0xFFFFFFFF (-1). Apply existing fixWavHeader logic to the chat completions path (was only used by ElevenLabs TTS route).

Also set correct Content-Type (audio/wav for WAV, audio/mpeg otherwise) instead of always returning audio/mpeg.

Fixes #11951

gist.headline: Waving goodbye to broken audio
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where WAV audio files from the chat completions endpoint were missing their header sizes. Your audio players will now happily accept our base64-encoded WAVs without throwing a tantrum.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue in the /v1/chat/completions endpoint where base64-encoded WAV files were returned with invalid RIFF and data chunk sizes (0xFFFFFFFF) and an incorrect audio/mpeg Content-Type.
gist.impact: Audio players and libraries will now correctly parse and play WAV files generated by the chat completions endpoint, and the Content-Type header accurately reflects the audio/wav format.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as an audio engineer, fixing a sound wave or a retro speaker. The robot companion listening happily to the fixed audio.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny headphones and using a small wrench to fix a glowing lime green (#ecf874) sound wave coming from a retro boombox. A chunky pixel robot companion with a monitor head happily listens nearby. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
